# claude-skill-c4-reverse-engineer

Claude Code skill that reverse-engineers C4 architecture diagrams (Context, Container, Component) and a behavioral specification from a codebase. Produces documentation of WHAT the system does, not how it's coded.

## Overview

- Generates L1 (Context), L2 (Container), and L3 (Component) C4 diagrams in Mermaid notation
- Produces a behavioral specification covering lifecycle, state machines, event flows, degradation paths, and platform-specific behavior
- Scales approach by codebase size: direct reads for small projects, subagent-based exploration for large ones
- Verifies every behavioral claim against source code before writing it into a deliverable
- Includes helper scripts for deterministic inventory of external calls and platform conditionals

## Installation

This skill must be symlinked into `~/.claude/skills/` — not copied. Symlinks keep the
installed skill in sync with the repo checkout and avoid stale copies.

See [jewzaam/claude-skills](https://github.com/jewzaam/claude-skills) for the
recommended approach to safely symlinking skills.

## Usage

```
/c4-reverse-engineer
```

The skill prompts for purpose (onboarding, audit, refactor prep, etc.) and which artifacts to produce, then works through 7 phases: intake, codebase survey, parallel exploration, structured verification, diagram generation, spec generation, and cross-artifact consistency check.

Output is written to `docs/c4/` in the target project directory.

## Helper Scripts

All scripts are standalone Python 3 (stdlib only, no dependencies).

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `scripts/count_source_lines.py` | Count non-test source lines per language, recommend a tier |
| `scripts/find_external_calls.py` | Enumerate subprocess, network, and filesystem calls |
| `scripts/find_platform_conditionals.py` | Enumerate platform-conditional code paths |

```bash
./scripts/count_source_lines.py /path/to/project
./scripts/find_external_calls.py /path/to/project --group
./scripts/find_platform_conditionals.py /path/to/project
```
